title: nie-daemon tests: auth, whoami, send, ws events integration
description:
**What:** Write integration tests for nie-daemon covering token auth rejection, /api/whoami, /api/send, and /ws/events event delivery end-to-end via a test relay.

**Algorithm:**
1. Create daemon/tests/integration.rs
2. Add test deps to daemon/Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies]: reqwest={version='0.12', features=['json']}, tokio-tungstenite.workspace, portpicker='1' (for free port selection)
3. Test helper: async fn start_test_daemon() -> (SocketAddr, String, DaemonGuard) — spawns daemon with fresh tmp identity + test relay; returns (addr, token, guard that stops daemon on drop)
4. Test: test_auth_rejected_no_token:
   - start daemon; GET /api/whoami without Authorization header
   - assert HTTP 401
5. Test: test_auth_rejected_wrong_token:
   - start daemon; GET /api/whoami with 'Authorization: Bearer wrong-token'
   - assert HTTP 401
6. Test: test_whoami_ok:
   - start daemon; GET /api/whoami with valid token
   - assert HTTP 200, body.pub_id.len()==64, body.pub_id chars all in '0-9a-f'
7. Test: test_send_returns_message_id:
   - start daemon; POST /api/send { text: 'integration test' } with valid token
   - assert HTTP 200, body.message_id length==36, body.message_id[8]=='-'
8. Test: test_ws_auth_rejected:
   - connect WS /ws/events without token → HTTP 401 (no upgrade)
9. Test: test_ws_event_delivery:
   - start daemon, connect WS /ws/events with valid token
   - POST /api/send { text: 'hello' } from another reqwest client
   - assert WS receives JSON event within 5 seconds: type=='message_received', text=='hello'
   - Use tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), ...) — never an infinite wait

**Defensive programming rules:**
- Tests must generate fresh identity per run (not hardcoded pub_id)
- WS event test has 5s timeout — not an infinite wait
- UUID format check uses structural assertion (length + hyphen positions), not regex magic
- Test relay uses random port (portpicker) to avoid conflicts

**Agent team:** Implementer (reads relay/tests/ for test relay spawn pattern) + Validator.

**Tests:** These are the tests — each named above with independent oracle (HTTP spec, UUID spec).

Notes: If test relay spawn pattern is complex, reuse spawn_relay helper from relay/tests/stress.rs or create a lightweight stub relay for unit testing.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-daemon passes all integration tests; no test hangs (all have timeouts); cargo clippy -- -D warnings exits 0
close_reason: integration tests pass: auth rejection, whoami, send 503, WS connect+event delivery — 14 tests total
